event: lives in a rural mountainous region
past medical history of a ruptured abdominal aortic aneurysm
history of smoking ending 40 years prior
discharged home with prescriptions for warfarin, digoxin, and diuretic therapy
presented to a local hospital emergency room
one-day history of shortness of breath
one week of a nonproductive cough
three weeks of malaise, fatigue and weakness
chest x-ray showed a left lower pulmonary infiltrate
treated with an infusion of dopamine
became acutely unstable and was intubated
transferred to a referral hospital by helicopter
irregular rhythm with no murmurs or gallops
mild edema of the lower extremities up to the knees
no pulses palpable distal to the femoral pulses
chest x-ray showed a left lower lobe infiltrate
right mid and upper zone infiltrates
empirical antibiotic therapy was started with gentamicin and ticarcillin/clavulanate
central venous pressure of 7 mm Hg
pulmonary artery pressure of 32/21 mm Hg
pulmonary artery wedge pressure of 19 mm Hg

blood cultures obtained on admission to the referral hospital yielded Streptococcus pneumoniae
gross and microscopic features of the gray hepatization stage of a pneumococcal pneumonia 4 to 7 days old
near-lobar early organizing pneumonia of the lower left lobe, with an associated 700 ml empyema
near-total old occlusion of the left anterior descending and left circumflex coronary arteries
